title: Rinorea lankawiensis
intro: Rinorea lankawiensis, also known as the Lankawi wood-vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Violaceae. This plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which make it a potential remedy for various ailments such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Rinorea lankawiensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Rinorea lankawiensis is often prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Rinorea lankawiensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Violaceae, commonly known as the violet family. It belongs to the genus Rinorea, which consists of tropical and subtropical species found in Asia and the Pacific. Rinorea lankawiensis is native to Langkawi Island in Malaysia and is known for its unique characteristics. The plant is a climber with small, star-shaped flowers and is often found growing in shaded areas. Rinorea lankawiensis is considered an endangered species due to habitat loss and degradation.
